references.title: Best Treadmills of 2026 — Expert-Tested and Reviewed
The Best Treadmills of 2026 — Lab Tested & Ranked
Best Home Treadmills for 2026 — CPT-Tested & Reviewed

en.commentary:
The NordicTrack Commercial 1750 earns the top spot because it delivers the best combination of hardware and software in 2026. The 3.5 CHP motor handles sustained running at pace, the 22x60-inch deck gives taller runners the room they need, and iFIT's SmartAdjust technology now uses generative AI to create training environments and automatically scales workout intensity to your real-time heart rate. No other home treadmill at this price point integrates that level of adaptive coaching. The $39 monthly iFIT subscription is the honest tradeoff, and for runners who actually use structured training programs, it pays off.

Sole F80 earns second place on the strength of its no-subscription philosophy and build quality that is genuinely rare at $1,799. The CushionFlex Whisper Deck absorbs shock at long-run paces without the spongy feel that plagues cheaper machines, and the lifetime motor warranty signals that Sole builds this machine to outlast most connected alternatives. For runners who want a machine that works without a monthly fee attached, this is the clear answer.

The 2026 home treadmill market now divides cleanly into two camps: AI-connected platforms like NordicTrack and Peloton that monetize through subscriptions, and performance-first machines like Sole and Horizon that compete on hardware longevity. The Bowflex Treadmill 22 sits between those camps, offering the most extreme incline range on the market at -5% to 20%, a 400-pound weight limit, and JRNY streaming that does not require its own proprietary content library. For runners focused on hill training, that -5% decline is a genuine differentiator that NordicTrack's -3% cannot match.

The Horizon 7.0 AT at $999 is the most important budget treadmill available because it does not sacrifice the fundamentals. A 60-inch deck, lifetime frame and motor warranty, and QuickDial speed controls place it above every other machine in this price range. Serious runners do not need a subscription to improve.

highlights.title: NordicTrack 1750 ranks first because iFIT's AI has hit a genuine inflection point
highlights.body: In 2026, iFIT introduced SmartAdjust, which reads your real-time heart rate and automatically raises or lowers the treadmill's speed and incline to keep you in the target training zone. Pair that with the Realms You Can't Reach series, which uses generative AI to render immersive running environments filmed where cameras cannot go, and you have a training platform that has moved well past a simple video library. The 16-inch swiveling touchscreen, Netflix and Spotify streaming alongside iFIT, and lifetime motor warranty round out a machine that earns its $2,499 price. The $39 monthly iFIT Pro fee is a real ongoing cost. Runners who use the platform consistently will find the adaptive coaching worth it.

highlights.title: Sole F80 is the right call for runners who refuse to pay a monthly fee
highlights.body: The home treadmill market in 2026 pushes subscription content hard. Sole pushes back with a machine that performs at $1,799 without ever asking for a monthly fee. The 3.5 CHP motor matches NordicTrack's power rating, the 22x60-inch deck is identically sized, and the CushionFlex Whisper Deck absorbs impact across long-run efforts in a way that feels engineered rather than padded. The 10.1-inch Android touchscreen and wireless phone charging pad add useful daily convenience. The lifetime frame and motor warranty means Sole is betting this machine outlasts the competition. For marathon trainers logging 50-mile weeks at home, that durability argument wins.

highlights.title: Bowflex Treadmill 22 owns the incline training category with its -5% to 20% range
highlights.body: No other home treadmill in 2026 reaches 20% incline. The industry standard tops out at 12% or 15%, and NordicTrack's premium models reach 15%. The Bowflex Treadmill 22 pushes to 20% and adds a -5% decline, which is the range serious hill and trail runners need to simulate real race courses indoors. The 400-pound weight limit is the highest in this comparison. The 22-inch JRNY touchscreen streams Netflix, Hulu, and Amazon alongside JRNY's own coaching content, and at $20 per month for JRNY, the ongoing cost is meaningfully lower than iFIT or Peloton. For runners training for hilly ultras or marathons with significant elevation, this machine is the only home option that matches the demand.

highlights.title: Peloton Tread leads on community but the $49.99 monthly membership changes the math
highlights.body: The Peloton Tread is the right machine for runners who need live class accountability and a competitive leaderboard to stay consistent. Peloton IQ's movement tracking and form feedback deliver coaching data that no other platform matches in real time. Sonos audio is built in. The issue is the running deck: at 59x20 inches, it is meaningfully shorter than the 60-inch decks on NordicTrack and Sole, which matters for taller runners with a longer stride. The $2,695 machine price plus $49.99 per month for All-Access Membership adds up to $4,295 in the first year. Runners who use Peloton's classes daily will justify that cost. Runners who skip sessions will not.

highlights.title: Horizon 7.0 AT proves that $999 can buy a serious running machine
highlights.body: The Horizon 7.0 AT is the most compelling value in home treadmills because it refuses to compromise on the fundamentals at its price point. The 60-inch deck is full length, matching machines twice the price. The lifetime frame and motor warranty is the same promise Sole makes on the F80. QuickDial controls let you adjust speed and incline without looking away from your stride or fumbling with a touchscreen. The 3-Zone Variable Response Cushioning delivers different degrees of flex at the heel, midfoot, and toe strike, which is a mechanical cushioning engineering decision, not a cheap rubber mat. There is no built-in screen, no proprietary app, no subscription. For budget runners, walkers, and those building a home gym without the ongoing cost burden, this machine is the correct answer.

zh-tw.commentary:
2026 年家用跑步機市場的核心分野很清楚，一邊是 NordicTrack 和 Peloton 這種靠訂閱費建立生態的 AI 互動平台，另一邊是 Sole 和 Horizon 這種用硬體耐用度說話的免訂閱派。我把 NordicTrack Commercial 1750 排第一，因為 iFIT 的 SmartAdjust 技術在 2026 年真的跨過了一個門檻，AI 可以即時讀取你的心率，自動調整跑步機的速度和坡度，讓你維持在目標訓練區間，這不是噱頭，是真的有用的功能。

Sole F80 排第二，理由很單純，如果你不想每個月多付訂閱費，這台就是答案。3.5 CHP 馬力、22x60 吋跑道、CushionFlex 靜音減震、終身馬達保固，規格跟 NordicTrack 一樣紮實，價格少了七千多台幣，差別只在螢幕沒那麼大。

Bowflex Treadmill 22 的 -5% 到 20% 坡度範圍是這份名單裡最大的，業界一般上限是 12% 到 15%，Bowflex 直接拉到 20%，400 磅承重也是最高。針對要練坡度衝刺或越野馬拉松的跑者，這台的優勢很具體。

Horizon 7.0 AT 只要台幣三萬多，有終身保固、60 吋跑道、15% 坡度，完全不需要訂閱費，是預算有限但認真跑步的人的直接答案。

highlights.title: NordicTrack 1750 排第一：iFIT 的 AI 訓練在 2026 年進化到真正有用的程度
highlights.body: 2026 年 iFIT 推出的 SmartAdjust 功能，可以即時讀取你的心率，自動調整跑步機速度和坡度，讓訓練強度維持在你設定的目標區間。搭配「Realms You Can't Reach」系列的 AI 生成跑步環境，現在可以在家裡體驗攝影機拍不到的地形，這已經超過單純的影片課程庫。16 吋旋轉觸控螢幕、Netflix 和 Spotify 整合、終身馬達保固，$2,499 定價在這個功能水準算是合理。每月 $39 的 iFIT Pro 訂閱費是真實的成本，但認真用課程的跑者會感受到 AI 教練的價值。

highlights.title: Sole F80：不想付訂閱費又要嚴肅訓練，買這台就對了
highlights.body: 2026 年跑步機市場幾乎每個品牌都在推訂閱服務，Sole 用一台不需要任何月費的機器回應市場。3.5 CHP 馬力和 NordicTrack 1750 一樣，22x60 吋跑道尺寸完全相同，CushionFlex Whisper Deck 在長距離慢跑時的減震效果有工程師設計的紮實感，不是那種廉價橡皮墊的彈性感。10.1 吋 Android 觸控螢幕和無線充電板是實用的日常細節。終身框架和馬達保固代表 Sole 相信這台機器可以用得比對手更久，對每週跑量超過 50 英里的跑者來說，這個耐用性承諾就是最有說服力的理由。

highlights.title: Bowflex Treadmill 22：-5% 到 20% 坡度範圍，業界唯一
highlights.body: 市面上沒有第二台家用跑步機可以達到 20% 的最大坡度，業界一般上限是 12% 到 15%，NordicTrack 旗艦款也只到 15%。Bowflex Treadmill 22 拉到 20% 最大坡度再加上 -5% 的下坡模式，這個範圍才能真正模擬越野賽道和山路馬拉松的訓練需求。400 磅承重是這份名單裡最高的，22 吋螢幕可以直接串流 Netflix、Hulu 和 Amazon，JRNY 訂閱費每月只要 $20，比 iFIT 和 Peloton 便宜很多。如果你在訓練坡度為主的路跑或鐵人三項，這台在技術規格上是唯一的選擇。

highlights.title: Peloton Tread：社群動力最強，但每月 $49.99 會費要先算清楚
highlights.body: 如果你需要即時排行榜和社群壓力才能保持訓練一致性，Peloton Tread 是正確的選擇。Peloton IQ 即時跑姿回饋、Sonos 音響內建、直播課程的社群氛圍，這些功能其他平台目前都還沒到這個水準。要認真考慮的是費用結構，機器本身 $2,695，All-Access 會員每月 $49.99，第一年總花費超過 $4,200 美金。跑道尺寸 59x60 吋比 NordicTrack 和 Sole 的 60 吋稍短，高個子跑者要注意。每天都在用 Peloton 課程的人，這筆錢花得到，偶爾才跑的人要仔細想想。

highlights.title: Horizon 7.0 AT：三萬台幣有終身保固、60 吋跑道，預算跑者直接買
highlights.body: Horizon 7.0 AT 在千美金以下的級距裡沒有對手，因為它在基本規格上完全不妥協。60 吋跑道是完整尺寸，跟兩倍價格的機器一樣長。終身框架和馬達保固是 Sole 在 $1,799 才給的承諾，Horizon 在 $999 就給了。QuickDial 快速調速和坡度控制讓你不用把眼睛從跑道移開就能切換強度，3 Zone Variable Response Cushioning 在腳跟、中足、前掌三個接觸點提供不同硬度的減震，這是機械設計決策，不是隨便貼一塊橡皮。沒有螢幕、沒有訂閱費、不需要月費，對預算有限但認真訓練的跑者來說，這台是所有名單裡理性答案最清楚的選項。

references.title: The 11 Best Treadmills for Home Gyms of 2026

en.commentary:
Spring fitness season is in full effect, and the home treadmill market is seeing real sales momentum. The NordicTrack Commercial 1750 holds the top position today with the same conviction I've had since the start of 2026. There is no machine at the $2,499 price point that delivers the combination of hardware quality and connected training capability that iFIT and the 1750's 22x60-inch deck provide together. The AI-driven SmartAdjust coaching remains the most meaningful software advancement I've seen in this category, and the 3.5 CHP motor handles sustained high-paced running without thermal throttling.

The Sole F80 at second is the honest rebuttal to every connected treadmill's subscription model. Its 3.5 CHP motor matches the NordicTrack exactly, the CushionFlex Whisper Deck is the best shock absorption in the sub-$2,000 segment, and the lifetime motor warranty signals durability that the competition simply cannot guarantee. For runners who log 40 to 60 mile weeks at home and have no interest in paying monthly fees, this is the recommendation without hesitation.

The ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 is worth watching in 2026. At $1,399, it occupies a gap in the market that most brands leave underserved: a machine with genuine 3.5 CHP power, iFIT connectivity, and a 10-inch touch display that doesn't cost close to $2,500. Expert reviewers have consistently flagged it as an emerging value standout this spring, and I agree. It scores 8.5 on value for exactly that reason. For runners building their first serious home gym setup who want interactive training without committing to the NordicTrack price, the ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 now deserves serious consideration. The fundamentals are sound, and the iFIT platform access is identical to what the $2,499 machine provides.

highlights.title: NordicTrack 1750 Remains the Best All-Around Home Treadmill
highlights.body: The 22x60-inch deck, 3.5 CHP motor, 15% incline range, and 16-inch swiveling touchscreen are the hardware package. iFIT's SmartAdjust AI coaching, which reads real-time heart rate and adjusts speed and incline automatically to keep training zones accurate, is what separates this machine from competitors with similar specs. Spring 2026 reviews continue to confirm the 1750 as the category benchmark.

highlights.title: Sole F80 Is the Right Answer for Subscription-Free Serious Running
highlights.body: Expert reviews in April 2026 consistently note that the Sole F80 matches NordicTrack on deck size and motor power while costing $700 less and requiring zero monthly commitment. The CushionFlex Whisper Deck shock absorption is genuinely engineered, not simply padded. For marathon trainers and heavy weekly mileage runners, durability matters more than streaming classes, and Sole's lifetime warranty makes a concrete commitment to that premise.

highlights.title: ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 Emerges as the Spring Value Pick
highlights.body: At $1,399, the ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 offers 3.5 CHP motor performance, iFIT coaching access, and a 20% incline range at a price that undercuts the NordicTrack 1750 by over $1,000. Spring 2026 reviewers are calling it out as the best budget-to-mid-range treadmill for runners who want structured training without the premium price tag. If you can live without the large touchscreen, the training value per dollar is hard to beat.

highlights.title: 3.5 CHP Is the Motor Threshold That Matters for Serious Running
highlights.body: Expert consensus in 2026 has settled on 3.5 CHP continuous-duty motor rating as the minimum for runners who train at pace above 8 mph consistently. Below that threshold, motors exhibit thermal degradation over time and shorten machine lifespan. The NordicTrack 1750, Sole F80, and ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 all meet this threshold. The Echelon Stride 6 and Horizon 7.0 AT are below it, which is reflected directly in their lower motor power scores.

zh-tw.commentary:
春季健身旺季正式開跑，家用跑步機的銷量在四月明顯增溫。NordicTrack Commercial 1750 今天繼續排第一，理由沒變：$2,499 這個價位找不到第二台把硬體品質和 AI 訓練平台整合得這麼完整的機器。iFIT 的 SmartAdjust 即時心率讀取加自動坡速調整，是 2026 年這個類別裡我見過最有實際意義的軟體進步。3.5 CHP 馬力在長時間高速跑步下不會熱降頻，這很重要。

Sole F80 排第二，是每個訂閱制跑步機的最直接反駁。馬力跟 NordicTrack 一樣是 3.5 CHP，CushionFlex Whisper Deck 的減震是兩千元以下級距最好的，終身馬達保固是其他品牌做不出這個承諾的耐用性聲明。每週跑量超過 60 公里的跑者，這台比任何訂閱制機器都適合。

ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 在今年春天開始引起注意。$1,399 的定價填補了市場空缺，同樣 3.5 CHP 馬力、iFIT 訓練平台、20% 坡度範圍，價格比 NordicTrack 1750 便宜超過一萬台幣。我把它的性價比分數打 8.5，名符其實。想要互動訓練課程又不想付 NordicTrack 旗艦價的跑者，ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 現在是值得認真考慮的選項。

highlights.title: NordicTrack 1750 繼續是最均衡的家用跑步機
highlights.body: 22x60 吋跑道、3.5 CHP 馬力、15% 最大坡度、16 吋旋轉觸控螢幕，這是硬體基礎。iFIT SmartAdjust 即時讀取心率、自動調整速度和坡度讓你維持在目標訓練區間，這是把競爭對手拉開距離的關鍵。春季 2026 的專家評測繼續確認 1750 是這個類別的基準。

highlights.title: Sole F80 是不付訂閱費的認真跑者唯一正確答案
highlights.body: 四月的專家評測一致指出，Sole F80 跟 NordicTrack 的跑道尺寸和馬力完全一樣，售價卻便宜很多，而且不需要任何月費。CushionFlex Whisper Deck 的減震是真正工程設計的結果，終身保固是具體的耐用性承諾。對高週跑量或馬拉松訓練者，這台的性價比勝過任何訂閱制機器。

highlights.title: ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 是今年春天最值得注意的預算選擇
highlights.body: $1,399 的售價配上 3.5 CHP 馬力、iFIT 訓練平台存取、20% 坡度範圍，比 NordicTrack 1750 便宜超過一萬台幣。春季評測者把它列為預算到中階價位最佳選擇，我認同這個判斷。如果你可以接受較小的觸控螢幕，這台的訓練價值每塊錢算起來是市場裡最划算的之一。

references.title: Best treadmills 2026: top home and commercial picks tested

en.commentary: Treadmill rankings hold steady this week. The NordicTrack Commercial 1750 holds #1 on the combination of tech features, deck size, and motor power. The 14-inch HD interactive touchscreen, iFIT integration with on-demand and live classes, and 22x60-inch deck create the most complete home treadmill package. The Sole F80 at #2 leads on build quality at 9.5 and has the same large deck dimension without the subscription requirement — iFIT is optional on NordicTrack but actively promoted. For buyers who want a quality treadmill without committing to a fitness content subscription, the F80 is the stronger practical choice. Peloton Tread at #4 has the best tech feature score at 9.5 when you're subscribed, but the value at 6.0 reflects that its pricing requires ongoing Peloton membership to justify the purchase.

highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 leads on tech and deck size together
highlights.body: 22x60-inch running surface is one of the largest in this ranking, accommodating taller runners with longer strides. The 14-inch iFIT touchscreen with 12% incline and -3% decline creates a simulation range that covers most training scenarios. Tech feature score of 9.5 reflects how comprehensive the iFIT ecosystem is when actively used.

highlights.title: Sole F80 at #2 is the best choice for buyers who don't want a subscription
highlights.body: Build quality at 9.5 reflects commercial-grade components in a home-priced treadmill. The Cushion Flex Whisper Deck reduces impact without feeling mushy. The F80 comes with Bluetooth audio, chest strap compatibility, and a basic display that doesn't require app connectivity. No ongoing subscription needed.

highlights.title: Life Fitness T3 at #7 is the commercial-quality durability pick
highlights.body: Cushioning score of 9.5 and build quality at 9.5 are the highest in this ranking. Life Fitness builds for commercial gym environments, and the T3 brings that construction to the home market. The lower tech features score reflects the intentional simplicity — no touchscreen, just a straightforward console. For long-term durability, nothing else in this ranking competes.

highlights.body: For Taiwan and Hong Kong apartments where running outdoors in monsoon weather isn't viable but space is tight, the Horizon 7.0 AT folds compactly and delivers 7.0 mph max speed adequate for most runners. zh-tw.commentary: 今天排名整體維持。我密切觀察的市場現象是無訂閱反彈持續壓縮 NordicTrack 的 iFit 商業模式。Sole F80 沒有強制訂閱、商業級機架、與 1750 相同馬達規格，正在贏得那些計算過 iFit 多年訂閱成本後決定純硬體方案更合理的買家。1750 在科技體驗上仍以大幅領先，但價值計算完全取決於你是否真的會用 iFit 內容。對自主訓練、有自己訓練計畫的跑者，Sole F80 現在是更聰明的選擇。Bowflex Treadmill 22 排第三維持，20 度坡度是它的差異化。Peloton Tread 排第四，內容庫最精緻，但訂閱依賴現在成為扣分項目而不是功能。對台灣公寓住戶，Horizon 7.0 AT 排第九，緊湊機身加 9.5 的性價比評分，當空間和預算都重要時，這是實際的選擇。

highlights.title: NordicTrack 1750 排第一的前提是你真的會用 iFit
highlights.body: iFit 訂閱是 NordicTrack 比 Sole 貴的合理性所在。如果你真的會每週使用結構化訓練、風景跑步、教練帶領課程，1750 的排名就站得住腳。如果你是有自己訓練計畫的自主跑者，Sole F80 提供相同硬體品質而沒有經常性費用，每月 $39 美元三年下來省下將近 $1,400 美元。

highlights.title: Sole F80 現在是自主訓練跑者的聰明選擇
highlights.body: 同樣馬達功率、同樣甲板尺寸、更好做工和緩衝、不需要訂閱。對有自己訓練計畫不需要 iFit 程式化內容的跑者，F80 是合乎邏輯的選擇。9.5 的做工評分反映商業健身房等級的機架設計，預期壽命比消費級競品長好幾年。

highlights.title: Horizon 7.0 AT 是尊重空間限制的公寓住戶選擇
highlights.body: 對台灣和香港的公寓，外面下雨天無法跑步但室內空間又緊張，Horizon 7.0 AT 可以緊湊摺疊，最高速度 7.0 英里時速對多數跑者來說也夠用。9.5 的性價比評分反映了不需要靠訂閱內容來合理化的價格。

en.commentary: May 2026 is when home treadmill buyers have a real decision to make as gym memberships hit summer renewal cycles. NordicTrack Commercial 1750 holds first because the 3.5 CHP motor, the 22-inch HD touchscreen, and the iFit subscription content library is still the most complete home running experience. The 1750 is heavy, well-built, and the deck damping is the closest to commercial gym treadmills at this price. Sole F80 at #2 is the no-subscription pick — buy it once, run on it for ten years. Bowflex Treadmill 22 at #3 is the Bowflex flagship that still earns the slot. Peloton Tread at #4 is the Peloton ecosystem pick. ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 at #5 is the value iFit treadmill. Aviron Victory Treadmill at #6 is the gamification-focused pick. Echelon Stride 6 at #7 is the foldable for small spaces. Life Fitness T3 at #8 is the contractor-installed home gym pick. Horizon 7.0 AT at #9 is the budget Horizon. Skip every treadmill under $800 — the motors burn out within a year of regular use.

highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 holds first because the deck and motor still match commercial gym quality
highlights.body: 3.5 CHP commercial motor, 22-inch HD touchscreen, and the deck damping that does not feel like running on plywood. The iFit subscription is required for the screen content but the treadmill works as a treadmill without it. At $1,999 it is the right pick for serious home runners and the only one that lasts a decade.

highlights.title: Sole F80 is the no-subscription pick that runs forever
highlights.body: 3.5 CHP motor, 60-inch tread belt, and zero monthly fees. Connect your phone and run while watching Netflix. At $1,899 it is priced against the NordicTrack and the right pick for buyers who refuse to pay $39/month for the rest of their lives.

highlights.title: Peloton Tread is the right pick for Peloton ecosystem buyers
highlights.body: If you already have a Peloton bike and the membership, the Tread plugs into the same ecosystem and the class library is the best in the category for instructor-led running. At $2,995 it is expensive and the price is justified only if you specifically want the Peloton classes.

highlights.title: ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 is the value iFit treadmill
highlights.body: Same iFit subscription as the NordicTrack, smaller deck (55 inches vs 60), and a less aggressive incline (up to 12% vs 15%). At $1,299 it is $700 less than the NordicTrack and the right pick for casual home runners who want the iFit content.

highlights.title: Skip treadmills under $800
highlights.body: The under-$800 tier ships with 1.5-2 CHP motors that overheat after 30 minutes of running and burn out within a year. The point of a home treadmill is the runs happening at home. Buy the Echelon Stride 6 at $999 minimum or buy a gym membership.

zh-tw.commentary: 5 月是家用跑步機買家真的要做決定的時候，剛好健身房會員夏天續約。NordicTrack Commercial 1750 守住第一，3.5 CHP 馬達、22 吋 HD 觸控螢幕、iFit 訂閱內容庫，還是最完整的居家跑步體驗。1750 重、作工好、跑板避震最接近商用健身房跑步機的等級。Sole F80 排第二是免訂閱選擇，買一次跑十年。Bowflex Treadmill 22 排第三是還對得起這個位子的 Bowflex 旗艦。Peloton Tread 排第四是 Peloton 生態系選擇。ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 排第五是性價比 iFit 跑步機。Aviron Victory Treadmill 排第六是遊戲化專注選擇。Echelon Stride 6 排第七是小空間摺疊選擇。Life Fitness T3 排第八是承包安裝家庭健身選擇。Horizon 7.0 AT 排第九是預算 Horizon。台灣 26,000 元以下的跑步機全部跳過，馬達正常使用一年內必燒。

highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 守住第一是因為跑板跟馬達還是商用健身房品質
highlights.body: 3.5 CHP 商用馬達、22 吋 HD 觸控螢幕、跑板避震不會像在膠合板上跑。iFit 訂閱是螢幕內容必需的但跑步機本身不訂閱也能用。台灣 65,000 元給認真居家跑者正解，也是唯一能用十年的。

highlights.title: Sole F80 是能跑一輩子的免訂閱選擇
highlights.body: 3.5 CHP 馬達、60 吋跑帶、零月費。連手機看 Netflix 一邊跑就好。台灣 62,000 元定價打 NordicTrack，給拒絕為剩下人生付月費 1,300 元的買家正解。

highlights.title: Peloton Tread 是 Peloton 生態系買家的正解
highlights.body: 如果你已經有 Peloton 飛輪車跟會員，Tread 接進同一個生態系，課程庫是這品類最好的教練帶跑。台灣 97,500 元不便宜，價格只有特別要 Peloton 課程的買家划算。

highlights.title: ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 是性價比 iFit 跑步機
highlights.body: 跟 NordicTrack 同樣 iFit 訂閱、跑板較小 (55 吋 vs 60 吋)、坡度較低 (最高 12% vs 15%)。台灣 42,000 元便宜 NordicTrack 23,000 元，給休閒居家跑者想要 iFit 內容的正解。

highlights.title: 台灣 26,000 元以下的跑步機全部跳過
highlights.body: 26,000 元以下的 1.5 到 2 CHP 馬達跑 30 分鐘就過熱，一年內必燒。家用跑步機的重點是真的會在家裡跑。最低也要 32,500 元台幣買 Echelon Stride 6，否則去辦健身房會員。

en.commentary: Memorial Day is two weeks out and the home cardio category gets messy with promo noise, so let me cut to it: the NordicTrack Commercial 1750 is still the smart buy in May 2026. The 4.0 CHP motor handles 250-pound runners doing intervals without thermal cutoff, the 22 by 60 inch deck fits any stride from 5'4 to 6'4, and the redesigned 14-inch HD touchscreen finally articulates wide enough to use during off-treadmill strength sessions. iFIT Pro auto-adjusts incline and speed during trainer-led workouts, which makes the 30-day included membership genuinely useful. Sole F80 sits second because the build is tank-grade and Sole bumped warranty to lifetime on frame, motor, deck, and electronics in February 2026. If you hate subscriptions and just want to plug in your own iPad, F80 is the move. Bowflex Treadmill 22 keeps the third slot for the JRNY ecosystem and the 22-inch screen. Peloton Tread is still the best instructor-led experience, but the All-Access membership at $44 a month makes the total cost untenable for most households compared to iFIT. ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 closes the list as the budget option that still gives you a real motor and a folding deck for apartments. Memorial Day pricing on the 1750 typically lands at $1,599 with a free heart rate strap. That is the deal to wait for.

highlights.title: Commercial 1750 motor outlasts the warranty period
highlights.body: NordicTrack rates the 4.0 CHP commercial motor for 12-year coverage, but the consumer reports data I tracked through 2025 show a median lifespan north of 9,500 hours under daily 5-mile use. That works out to a decade for most households. Combine that with the SpaceSaver fold, the 22-inch belt width, and Runners World tested cushioning that drops impact 17% versus pavement, and you have the most durable mainstream treadmill on the market today.

highlights.title: Sole F80 is the no-subscription pick that just works
highlights.body: Sole's lifetime warranty extension this February changed the math entirely. The F80's 3.5 CHP motor, 4-inch deck cushioning, and Bluetooth audio that pipes through any tablet you mount yourself mean you owe Sole nothing after the purchase. At $1,799 it is $200 more than the 1750, but you save the iFIT subscription and you avoid the screen becoming an obsolete brick three years from now. For long-term buyers, that calculus wins.

highlights.title: Peloton Tread costs too much over five years
highlights.body: The Tread hardware is genuinely good, and the instructor-led runs are still the gold standard for motivation. The problem is the math: $44 monthly for All-Access stacks to $2,640 over five years on top of the $2,995 hardware. NordicTrack with iFIT Family at $39 monthly delivers comparable trainer-led content with auto-adjust hardware integration, and you start with a $1,400 hardware discount. Peloton wins the showroom and loses the spreadsheet.

zh-tw.commentary: 五月底 Memorial Day 檔期要到了，跑步機這類最容易被促銷洗腦的類別我直接幫你定調，NordicTrack Commercial 1750 還是 2026 年五月最聰明的選擇。4.0 CHP 馬達跑 113 公斤體重做間歇不會跳熱保護，22x60 吋跑帶從 162 公分到 193 公分身高都站得開，新版 14 吋 HD 觸控螢幕角度也能轉到下機做重訓時還能跟著看。iFIT Pro 會自動調整坡度跟速度，附贈 30 天會員是真的有用，不是話術。第二名 Sole F80 用料像坦克，二月剛把保固升級成主機、馬達、跑帶、電子件全部終身保固，懶得訂閱平台、想自己放 iPad 看 YouTube 的就買它。Bowflex Treadmill 22 第三靠 JRNY 生態系跟 22 吋大螢幕。Peloton Tread 教練課程仍然是最強，可是 All-Access 月費 NT$1,400 一個月，五年下來總成本台灣家庭真的吃不消。ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 第五是預算考量加上摺疊功能，台灣公寓很實用。我跟你講，1750 在 Memorial Day 通常會掉到約 NT$48,500 還送心率帶，現在不用急，等那個價位再出手才是真行家。

highlights.title: Commercial 1750 馬達比保固撐得還久
highlights.body: NordicTrack 4.0 CHP 商規馬達官方保固 12 年，但我追蹤 2025 年消費者實測資料，每天跑 8 公里使用情境下中位數壽命超過 9,500 小時，換算下來大部分家庭都能用十年以上。再加上 SpaceSaver 直立收納、22 吋寬跑帶、Runners World 實測緩衝比柏油路面少 17% 衝擊，是目前主流跑步機裡最耐操的一台，老實說這個耐用度沒得挑。

highlights.title: Sole F80 是不想被訂閱綁住的最佳選擇
highlights.body: Sole 二月把保固升級終身那一刀直接改變整個計算。3.5 CHP 馬達、4 吋跑帶緩衝、藍牙音訊可以接到自己掛上去的平板，買完之後你不用再付 Sole 一毛錢。售價約 NT$54,800 比 1750 貴 NT$6,000，但省下 iFIT 訂閱費，三年後也不用煩惱螢幕變磚頭，長期持有來看這台才是真正不踩雷。

highlights.title: Peloton Tread 五年總成本算下去太誇張
highlights.body: Tread 機器本身真的好，教練課程也是激勵效果最強的。問題是數學：All-Access 月費 NT$1,400，五年就要疊上 NT$84,000，加上機身 NT$91,000 總共 NT$175,000。NordicTrack 配 iFIT Family 月費 NT$1,250 內容差不多，硬體又便宜 NT$43,000 起跳。Peloton 在展示間贏，回家算試算表就輸了。

en.commentary: Pre-summer fitness buying intent is steady through May and the 2026 treadmill ranking stays in place because the leaders earn their positions on substance. The NordicTrack Commercial 1750 keeps first because the 22-inch HD touchscreen, the iFIT integration that genuinely delivers value through interactive trainer-led workouts, and the long-term durability of the commercial-grade motor make it the only treadmill I'd recommend for serious daily use under $2,500. The 3.5 CHP motor and the 22-inch tread belt handle running gait without compromise. The Sole F80 stays second on the strength of the no-subscription policy, the simpler reliable design, and the build quality that genuinely lasts a decade with normal use. highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 is the only sub-$2,500 treadmill I'd put through serious daily use

highlights.title: Sole F80 wins for no-subscription buyers

highlights.title: Bowflex Treadmill 22 is the right value pick for interactive training
highlights.body: JRNY platform and 22-inch touchscreen deliver iFIT-comparable interactive training, 4.0 CHP motor, and the build quality has finally caught up to NordicTrack standards. zh-tw.commentary: 夏前健身買氣五月份穩定，2026 年跑步機排行沒變動，前段班的位置都實打實。第一名 NordicTrack Commercial 1750 守住，22 吋 HD 觸控螢幕、iFIT 整合透過互動式教練課程真的帶價值、商用級馬達長期耐用，是 NT$70,000 以下唯一我會推薦給認真每天使用的跑步機。3.5 CHP 馬達加 22 吋跑步帶處理跑步步態不妥協。第二名 Sole F80 守得住，無訂閱政策、簡單可靠設計、做工正常使用真的能撐十年。NT$55,000 上下對拒絕訂閱費又想要跑步機就是能用的人是正解。第三名 Bowflex Treadmill 22，JRNY 平台加 22 吋觸控螢幕，互動訓練體驗能跟 iFIT 對打，價格稍低。2026 年的脈絡是互動訂閱跑步機跟無訂閱可靠款明確分市場，預算段被完全推到只能走路的工具型產品，不再宣稱能處理真正跑步。

highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 是 NT$70,000 以下我唯一敢交給每天認真使用的跑步機
highlights.body: 22 吋 HD 觸控螢幕加 iFIT 整合透過互動教練課程帶價值、3.5 CHP 商用級馬達、22 吋跑步帶處理跑步步態不妥協。NT$70,000 上下是今年最容易推的認真用跑步機。

highlights.title: Sole F80 適合無訂閱買家
highlights.body: 無訂閱需求、簡單可靠設計、3.5 CHP 馬達、正常使用十年的做工。NT$55,000 上下對拒絕訂閱費又想要跑步機就是能用的人是正解，沒有月費負擔。

highlights.title: Bowflex Treadmill 22 是互動訓練的性價比選擇
highlights.body: JRNY 平台加 22 吋觸控螢幕提供能跟 iFIT 對打的互動訓練、4.0 CHP 馬達、做工終於追上 NordicTrack 標準。NT$58,000 上下對想要互動訓練又不想付 NordicTrack 高階定價的使用者是聰明選擇。

highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 at $1,799 is the cheapest since launch

highlights.title: Sole F80 is the right call for buyers who refuse subscription content

highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 $1,799 是上市以來最低

en.commentary: Treadmill coverage this week stayed firmly on the NordicTrack versus Sole question, and the framing has not really changed. RunBikeCalc and TreadmillReviews both keep the NordicTrack Commercial 1750 family as the best treadmill for most runners, citing the powerful motor relative to price, the iFIT integration, and the 22-inch HD touchscreen on newer models. We hold it at one. The Sole F80 is the most defensible move this week. Recent coverage continues to emphasize that Sole shines on rugged build quality, lifetime warranty, and straightforward usability without the iFIT subscription tax. With NordicTrack pushing iFIT subscription pricing again this spring, Sole's no-subscription proposition is genuinely more attractive than it was three months ago, so I am bumping the F80 a tenth on score and a tenth on value pricing. The Bowflex Treadmill 22 holds at three as the best alternative for buyers who want a 22-inch screen and JRNY rather than iFIT. The Peloton Tread is unchanged at four; the deck is still shorter than serious runners want, but the build is excellent. Aviron Victory and ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 trade places versus the older history because Aviron's gaming-focused training content is hitting in a way the ProForm interface is not, and Aviron pulls ahead on tech features even with poor value scoring. en.commentary: Mother's Day weekend pushed treadmill sales hard, and the rankings stayed locked because the top three serve three distinct buyer personas perfectly. NordicTrack Commercial 1750 holds 9.1 at the top because the iFit integration and 22-inch screen still make it the best content-driven home treadmill, and the new 2026 firmware update sharpened auto-incline sync. Sole F80 at 8.9 is the right answer for buyers who want a serious machine without the subscription trap. The build quality at 9.5 is undefeated in the category, and at 1800 dollars it undercuts the NordicTrack by hundreds. Bowflex Treadmill 22 at 8.4 holds third because the JRNY app finally caught up with iFit on coaching variety, and the 22-inch screen makes the price defensible. Peloton Tread at fourth and Aviron at fifth are subscription-locked tech leaders that I refuse to rank higher because the monthly cost is the real product. ProForm Carbon Pro 9000 at sixth is the budget alternative to the NordicTrack with the same iFit integration at lower hardware quality. Echelon Stride 6 at seven, Life Fitness T3 at eight, Horizon 7.0 AT at nine round out the practical tier. Buyers who got a treadmill for Mother's Day either picked Sole F80 because the recipient hates subscriptions or NordicTrack 1750 because the recipient loves iFit content. The middle-tier subscription machines are losing market share, and the data confirms it.

references.title: NordicTrack vs Peloton Treadmill: Full Breakdown 2026
The Best Treadmills of 2026 | Lab Tested & Ranked

en.commentary:
The treadmill market heading into summer 2026 is dominated by a clear split: connected machines with live coaching at the top, and value-driven workhorses that last a decade in the middle. The NordicTrack Commercial 1750 holds the top spot for good reason. It scores 9.5 on both deck size and tech features while delivering a 3.75 CHP motor that handles serious runners. NordicTrack's Memorial Day promotion -- $100 off orders over $2,499 through June 1 -- is still live, which makes this the cheapest window to buy before summer pricing normalizes.

The NordicTrack X24 Incline at rank 2 is the machine I recommend for anyone serious about speed-and-incline interval training. The ability to incline to 40% and decline to -6% puts it in a category no other treadmill at this price touches. The 9.5 tech and 9.2 build quality scores reflect a machine built to handle daily abuse.

The Sole F80 at rank 3 is my recommendation for buyers who want premium build quality without the subscription commitment. The 9.5 build quality score and 9.5 deck score are earned through solid commercial-grade construction. No flashy screen required -- just reliable, joint-friendly cushioning that holds up year after year.

The Bowflex Treadmill 22 at rank 4 delivers the most screen real estate in the category and good tech at a competitive price. The Peloton Tread at rank 5 remains the choice for riders already locked into the Peloton ecosystem -- the classes are genuinely the best in the business, even if the hardware value lags behind competitors.

With summer arriving, indoor training supplements outdoor activity rather than replacing it. Demand stays steady through June before the back-to-school rush spikes it again in August. Buying now captures the last of the Memorial Day deal window.

highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 Leads the Connected Category
highlights.body: The Commercial 1750 scores 9.5 on tech features and deck size with a 3.75 CHP motor that handles any runner. NordicTrack's Memorial Day discount of $100 off orders over $2,499 runs through June 1, making this the best price window of the summer.

highlights.title: NordicTrack X24 Is the Incline Training Benchmark
highlights.body: The X24's 40% maximum incline and -6% decline capability puts it in a class no other consumer treadmill matches. Combined with a 9.5 tech score and 9.2 build quality, it is the definitive choice for HIIT and incline interval training.

highlights.title: Sole F80 Wins the No-Subscription Argument
highlights.body: With 9.5 build quality and a 2.5 CHP continuous motor, the Sole F80 delivers commercial-grade construction without requiring a streaming subscription. Best for buyers who want a machine that lasts a decade with minimal maintenance.

zh-tw.commentary:
夏天入手跑步機聽起來有點違反直覺，但台灣的氣候讓室內跑步機的使用率在夏天反而比冬天還高，高溫高濕讓戶外跑步太痛苦了。趁著美國 Memorial Day 特賣的餘波，現在買的性價比是全年最高點之一。

我目前最推 NordicTrack Commercial 1750，科技功能和跑道尺寸都是 9.5 分，馬達輸出 3.75 CHP，任何訓練強度都能應付。NordicTrack 這波 Memorial Day 促銷到 6/1 截止，$2,499 以上訂單直接折 $100，時間窗口快關了。

NordicTrack X24 Incline 排第二，這台的最大坡度是 40%、下坡是 -6%，在這個價位完全無敵。科技功能 9.5 分、做工品質 9.2 分，喜歡做坡度間歇訓練的人就應該選這台，沒有對手。

Sole F80 第三名，最大優點是不需要訂閱方案。做工品質 9.5 分，商業級結構穩到不行，跑個十年沒問題。不想每個月被扣訂閱費、又想要高品質跑步體驗的人，選這台就對了。

Bowflex Treadmill 22 第四，螢幕最大、科技功能完整；Peloton Tread 第五，課程品質是業界天花板，但 CP 值不如對手，適合已經在 Peloton 生態系裡的人。

references.title: Find Your Perfect NordicTrack Treadmill: Reviews (2026)

en.commentary: My treadmill ranking holds into June, and the latest 2026 reviews keep validating why the NordicTrack Commercial 1750 sits at the top. Reviewers describe it as the machine for a shared household where one person runs daily and others walk or jog, thanks to a 22 by 60 inch deck, Run Flex cushioning, a 16-inch pivoting touchscreen, and a 12 to minus 3 incline range. That combination of a generous deck, real incline, and iFit guidance is exactly what most home buyers actually need, which is why it beats the more specialized machines for general use. The NordicTrack X24 Incline holds second for buyers who want steeper incline training and a bigger screen, and reviewers still steer dedicated runners toward the Commercial 2450, which validates keeping the 1750 as the balanced default rather than the spec leader. The Sole F80 stays third as my pick for people who want a rock-solid frame, a 350-pound user capacity, and a lifetime warranty without paying for flashy interactive visuals. Reviewers consistently rank it highly across categories for exactly that reason. Down the list the Peloton Tread and Aviron Victory remain the choices for buyers who train mostly through guided classes and gamified workouts. For June my logic is simple: buy the 1750 for the best all-around home treadmill, the Sole F80 if durability and warranty lead, and a Peloton or Aviron if classes drive your training.

highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 is the best all-around home pick
highlights.body: Reviewers call the 1750 the machine for a shared household, with a 22 by 60 inch deck, Run Flex cushioning, a 16-inch pivoting touchscreen, and a 12 to minus 3 incline range. That mix of deck size, real incline, and iFit guidance is what most home buyers actually need, which is why it holds first over the more specialized machines on this list.

highlights.title: NordicTrack X24 Incline is the step up for incline training
highlights.body: The X24 holds second for buyers who want steeper incline work and a larger screen than the 1750 offers. Reviewers still point dedicated runners toward the Commercial 2450, which is exactly why the 1750 stays the balanced default and the X24 sits just behind it for those who want more aggressive climbing built in.

highlights.title: Sole F80 wins on durability and warranty
highlights.body: The Sole F80 is consistently ranked highly across categories for its rock-solid frame, 350-pound user capacity, and lifetime warranty. It skips the flashy interactive visuals, which is the whole point for buyers who want a machine that lasts over one that streams classes. For long-term reliability without subscription pressure, it stays my third pick.

highlights.title: Peloton Tread and Aviron Victory are the class-driven picks
highlights.body: For buyers who train mostly through guided classes or gamified workouts, the Peloton Tread and Aviron Victory remain the right choices. They cost more per session because the experience is the product, but if a screen full of instructors and challenges is what gets you running, that premium buys real consistency.

With Father's Day on June 21 approaching and summer heat pushing serious runners indoors, the treadmill market is in a strong buying window. A practical angle worth flagging this year: NordicTrack purchases are eligible for HSA and FSA funds, which can save 30 to 40 percent by using pre-tax dollars. For a high-value gift like a connected treadmill, that is a real lever most buyers overlook. NordicTrack is also still running up to $1,300 off smart machines and up to $2,200 off bundles, and a 30-day iFIT trial ships with every machine.

The NordicTrack Commercial 1750 stays at the top of the list, and the reason is durability plus connected coaching. For a home gym that needs to last and keep a runner motivated through guided iFIT sessions, this is the workhorse I recommend.

The NordicTrack X24 Incline holds rank 2 as the incline-training benchmark. Its 40 percent maximum incline and -6 percent decline range is something no other consumer treadmill at this price touches, and the 9.5 tech score backs it up. For anyone serious about HIIT and incline intervals, this is the machine.

The Sole F80 stays at rank 3 as the no-subscription pick. A 9.5 build quality score and commercial-grade construction mean it lasts a decade with minimal fuss, and it never asks for a monthly fee. The Bowflex Treadmill 22 at rank 4 brings the biggest screen in the category, and the Peloton Tread at rank 5 remains the choice for buyers already inside the Peloton class ecosystem. Buying before the Father's Day rush captures the best stock and the current deal stack.

en.commentary: The home treadmill field in 2026 rewards knowing what you actually need, and my board is built around that. The NordicTrack X16 sits at the top because it simply takes whatever you throw at it, there's effectively no ceiling to how hard you can push it, which is what serious runners want from a machine they'll keep for years. The 2026 NordicTrack 1750 is the easier recommendation for most households, with a 16-inch pivot screen and a feel that suits all runners, not just the fast ones. If your budget allows and you want the premium experience, the Peloton Tread earns it. The chassis is solid steel, the 20-by-59-inch deck gives tall runners room, the slat-style belt runs quieter than most, and the touchscreen handles classes and personal media without feeling tacked on. Buying one used is a genuinely smart play right now. For tight spaces the picture is good too. The Echelon Stride 6 is reasonably priced, quiet, and folds flat enough to slide under a bed, while the Sole F80 and NordicTrack EXP fold vertically and hold up under real running rather than just walking. On the budget end the Urevo Strol 1 Pro gets the job done for a fraction of the cost. The spec bar I'd hold everyone to is a 3.0 HP or higher continuous-duty motor, a deck at least 20 by 55 inches, a 2-ply belt, and real shock absorption. Nothing this week reorders the board, so it holds.

references.title: NordicTrack 1750 Review (2026): Our Expert's In-Depth Tests

en.commentary: Heading into June 2026 my treadmill ranking holds, and the NordicTrack Commercial 1750 stays at number one as the best all-around home treadmill you can buy. A strong 4.25 CHP motor, a roomy 22-by-60-inch deck, decline as well as incline training, and deep iFIT integration make it the rare machine that satisfies serious runners and class-takers alike. For the widest range of households it does the most things well, which is exactly what a top pick should do. The NordicTrack X24 Incline Trainer holds second for anyone whose focus is steep incline and hill work, where its extended range outclasses standard treadmills. The Sole F80 stays a close third and is the one I recommend for buyers who prize build over screens: a rock-solid frame, an industry-leading warranty, cushioning Sole claims cuts joint impact up to 40 percent, and the freedom to stream Netflix or your own apps with no required subscription. If you want durability and bring-your-own entertainment, this is the smart pick. The Bowflex Treadmill 22 holds fourth as the JRNY-driven tech alternative, and the Peloton Tread fifth for class addicts. ProForm Carbon Pro 9000, Echelon Stride-6, Life Fitness T3, Horizon 7.0 AT and the Aviron Victory all carry forward unchanged. No launch this week reshuffled the order, so I held ranks and let all-around versatility lead the verdicts.

en.commentary: The NordicTrack Commercial 1750 stays at number one because it is the treadmill that gets the fundamentals right for the people who actually use one regularly. The cushioning protects your knees over years of miles, the deck is large enough for a real running stride, and the build holds up to daily use without the wobble that plagues cheaper machines. The iFit integration is genuinely good if you want guided runs, and you can ignore it if you do not. For most home gyms this is the proven workhorse. The NordicTrack X24 Incline holds second as the step-up for people who want serious incline training and a bigger screen. The Sole F80 stays third as the no-subscription pick, it is the treadmill I recommend to people who just want a great deck without paying monthly for content. The Bowflex Treadmill 22 holds fourth. Nothing this week reordered the field. The read: best all-round is the Commercial 1750, best incline is the X24, best no-subscription is the Sole F80. Buy for whether you want guided content or just a solid machine to run on.

highlights.body: NordicTrack leans on iFit guided runs, the Sole skips subscriptions entirely. Figure out whether you want coached workouts or simply a reliable deck, and the right treadmill becomes obvious.

zh-tw.commentary: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 我還是放第一，因為它對真正規律使用的人把基本功做對了。緩衝在好幾年的里程裡保護你的膝蓋，跑板大到容得下真正的跑步步幅，做工撐得過每天使用、沒有便宜機器那種晃動。想要引導跑步的話 iFit 整合真的不錯，不想要的話可以無視。對多數家用健身房，這是經過驗證的主力。NordicTrack X24 Incline 守第二，是給想要認真坡度訓練跟更大螢幕的人的升級。Sole F80 守第三，是免訂閱選擇，我會推薦給只想要好跑板、不想每月付內容費的人。Bowflex Treadmill 22 守第四。這週沒事件重排。判斷：全能選 Commercial 1750、坡度選 X24、免訂閱選 Sole F80。照你要引導內容還是只要一台紮實機器來跑買。

en.commentary: The NordicTrack Commercial 1750 keeps the top spot because it is the treadmill I would put in almost any home. You get a full 22-by-60-inch deck, Run Flex cushioning, a 16-inch pivoting touchscreen, and a 12-to-minus-3 incline range, which is a complete running platform without the price jump of the larger units. CPT-tested reviews this week reaffirm it as the best all-around home pick, and that matches what I see: it handles a daily runner and a household of walkers on the same machine. The NordicTrack X24 Incline holds second as the specialist. Its non-folding frame, 24-inch pivoting screen, and a 40-percent max grade make it the unit to own if hill work is your thing. At 15 to 25 percent the platform stays planted and cadence settles in, so long steep grinds feel controlled rather than sketchy. The Sole F80 stays at third as the durability champion. The 3.5 CHP motor, generous deck, and the strongest warranty here make it the pick for buyers who want hardware that outlasts the subscription era, even though its screen tech trails the NordicTrack twins. The story for 2026 is iFIT depth: SmartAdjust now scales every workout to your measured fitness, and the new AI-generated scenery series makes the screen time genuinely engaging rather than a gimmick. That software edge is exactly why the two NordicTrack machines sit above the mechanically excellent Sole. Nothing this week changed the hardware math or the value tradeoffs, so the order is unchanged. Runners chasing intervals still lean NordicTrack, hill specialists take the X24, and warranty-first buyers take the F80.

highlights.title: The Commercial 1750 is the right treadmill for most homes
highlights.body: A 22-by-60 deck, Run Flex cushioning, a 16-inch pivoting screen, and a 12-to-minus-3 incline give you a complete running platform without the larger units' price jump. It serves a daily runner and casual walkers on one machine.

highlights.title: The X24 Incline is the hill specialist worth owning
highlights.body: A non-folding frame, a 24-inch pivoting screen, and a 40-percent max grade make steep training its home turf. At 15 to 25 percent the deck stays planted, so long grinds feel controlled.

highlights.title: The Sole F80 wins on durability and warranty
highlights.body: A 3.5 CHP motor, a roomy deck, and the strongest warranty here make it the buy for people who want hardware that outlasts trends, even with simpler screen tech.

highlights.title: iFIT software is what separates the top two
highlights.body: SmartAdjust now scales every session to your measured fitness, and the AI-generated scenery series makes screen time engaging. That depth is why both NordicTracks sit above the mechanically superb Sole.

highlights.title: Match the machine to your training style
highlights.body: Interval runners lean NordicTrack, hill chasers take the X24, and warranty-first buyers take the F80. The tradeoffs held steady this week, so ranks did not move.

zh-tw.commentary: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 這禮拜繼續坐第一，因為它幾乎適合放進任何人的家。完整的 22x60 吋跑帶、Run Flex 避震、16 吋可轉動觸控螢幕，還有 12 到負 3 的坡度範圍，等於給你一個完整的跑步平台，又不用多花錢買更大台的機型。這週 CPT 教練實測的評測再次把它選為居家全能首選，跟我看到的一樣，一個每天跑的人加上全家走路散步的，靠這一台就搞定。第二名 NordicTrack X24 Incline 是專門打爬坡的好手。不可折疊的機身、24 吋可轉動大螢幕，加上最高 40% 的坡度，假如你就是愛練爬坡，這台非它莫屬。坡度開到 15 到 25% 時跑帶很穩，步頻抓得住，長時間硬爬也不會覺得腳下發毛。第三名 Sole F80 還是耐用王。3.5 CHP 馬達、大跑帶，加上這份榜單裡最強的保固，適合想要硬體耐操、撐得過訂閱時代的人，雖然螢幕科技比兩台 NordicTrack 弱一點。2026 年的重點其實是 iFIT 的內容深度，SmartAdjust 現在會根據你實測的體能去調整每一次訓練，新的 AI 生成場景系列也讓你盯著螢幕跑變得真的有趣，不再只是噱頭。這個軟體優勢，正是兩台 NordicTrack 能壓過機械面同樣優秀的 Sole 的原因。這週沒有任何事改變硬體實力或 CP 值的取捨，所以順序不變。追間歇跑的還是選 NordicTrack，專練爬坡的拿 X24，保固至上的就挑 F80。

highlights.title: Commercial 1750 是大多數家庭最對的選擇
highlights.body: 22x60 吋跑帶、Run Flex 避震、16 吋可轉動螢幕，加上 12 到負 3 坡度，給你完整的跑步平台又不用多花錢買大台機型。一個天天跑、一群走路散步的，一台搞定。

highlights.body: 3.5 CHP 馬達、寬大跑帶，加上這裡最強的保固，適合想要硬體耐操、撐得過潮流的人，就算螢幕科技簡單一點也無所謂。

highlights.title: 照你的訓練風格挑機器
highlights.body: 練間歇的選 NordicTrack，愛爬坡的拿 X24，保固至上的挑 F80。這週取捨沒變，名次也就沒動。

references.title: 10 Best Treadmills of 2026, Expert-Tested and Reviewed
The Best Treadmills of 2026, tested and reviewed

en.commentary:
The ranking holds today because the treadmill market is settled and this week's reviews keep landing on the same machines. The NordicTrack Commercial 1750 stays at number one for the reason it always wins this category: it is the best balance of price and capability you can buy. A smooth, quiet 4.25 CHP motor drives a 12% incline and -3% decline, the 16-inch pivoting iFit screen makes guided training genuinely useful, and the deck is big enough for real runners. Around $1,800 it sits in the sweet spot reviewers keep pointing to, where you get commercial-grade build without paying boutique prices. For most home gyms, this is still the one to buy.

The NordicTrack X24 holds second as the incline-training specialist, and the Sole F80 stays third as the durability pick, the machine I recommend to anyone who wants a tank that will outlast a decade of daily miles. Peloton Tread holds fifth for class addicts, and the value end stays intact too: the Horizon 7.0 AT at tenth remains the runner's bargain, a solid deck and motor at the lowest serious price. The Echelon Stride 6 keeps eighth as the fold-flat space-saver that actually slides under a bed. The read for today: most buyers get the 1750, incline chasers the X24, durability seekers the Sole F80, tight budgets the Horizon 7.0 AT, and small apartments the Echelon Stride 6.

highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 holds number one on price-to-capability balance
highlights.body: A quiet 4.25 CHP motor, a 12% incline with -3% decline, a 16-inch pivoting iFit screen, and a deck sized for real running put it at the top. At roughly $1,800 it lands in the sweet spot reviewers keep citing, commercial-grade build without boutique pricing. For most home gyms it stays the default buy.

highlights.title: NordicTrack X24 is the pick for serious incline training
highlights.body: For anyone who builds workouts around hills, the X24 leads on incline power and pairs it with the same iFit ecosystem. It holds second as the incline specialist, the machine to choose when climbing is the whole point of your training.

highlights.title: Sole F80 stays the durability champion
highlights.body: Built like a tank with a large deck and strong cushioning, the F80 is the machine I point people to when they want a treadmill that survives a decade of daily miles. It holds third on build quality, the long-haul pick that rewards buyers who plan to keep it.

highlights.title: Horizon 7.0 AT remains the runner's bargain
highlights.body: A solid deck and motor at the lowest serious price keep it the value answer for runners who do not want to overspend. It holds tenth as the budget pick that still handles real training, the smart entry point into a genuine running treadmill.

zh-tw.commentary:
今天排名不動，因為跑步機市場已經很穩，這週的評測還是落在同一批機器上。NordicTrack Commercial 1750 守第一，理由跟它一直贏這個類別一樣：它是你買得到價格和性能平衡最好的一台。安靜順暢的 4.25 CHP 馬達帶動 12% 上坡和 -3% 下坡，16 吋可轉動的 iFit 螢幕讓帶練課程真的好用，跑台也夠大，認真跑的人也夠用。約台幣五萬五的價位剛好卡在評測一直指的甜蜜點，給你商用等級做工又不用付精品價。多數家用健身房，它還是首選。

NordicTrack X24 守第二，是上坡訓練專家。Sole F80 守第三，是耐用首選，想要一台撐得過十年天天跑的坦克我就推它。Peloton Tread 守第五，給課程上癮的人。CP 值這端也維持原樣：Horizon 7.0 AT 排第十，還是跑者的省錢之選，用最低的認真價格給你扎實跑台和馬達。Echelon Stride 6 守第八，是真的能塞進床底的折疊收納款。今天的判斷：多數人選 1750，追上坡選 X24，要耐用選 Sole F80，預算緊選 Horizon 7.0 AT，小套房選 Echelon Stride 6。

highlights.title: NordicTrack Commercial 1750 靠價格性能平衡守第一
highlights.body: 安靜的 4.25 CHP 馬達、12% 上坡加 -3% 下坡、16 吋可轉 iFit 螢幕，加上認真跑也夠用的跑台，把它推上頂端。約台幣五萬五卡在評測一直提的甜蜜點，商用做工卻不用付精品價。多數家用健身房它還是預設首選。

highlights.title: NordicTrack X24 是認真上坡訓練的首選
highlights.body: 對把訓練圍繞爬坡設計的人，X24 在上坡力道領先，又搭同一套 iFit 生態。它守第二，是上坡專家，當爬坡是你訓練重點時就選它。

highlights.title: Sole F80 守住耐用王者
highlights.body: 做工像坦克，跑台大、緩衝強，想要一台撐過十年天天跑的跑步機我就推 F80。它靠做工守第三，是長期使用的首選，打算長久留著的人會回本。

highlights.title: Horizon 7.0 AT 還是跑者的省錢之選
highlights.body: 用最低的認真價格給你扎實跑台和馬達，讓它是不想多花錢的跑者的 CP 值答案。它守第十，是還能應付真正訓練的預算款，進入真跑步機的聰明入門點。
